channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Photos of Everyday American Family Life in the 1950s from the David Wipf Collection
item.description: The 1950s marked a significant period in American history characterized by economic prosperity, suburban expansion, and a strong emphasis on family values following the end of World War II. This era e...

item.title: Edo Police Rescue All Remaining Six Kidnapped Passengers on Benin-Lagos Highway
item.description: The Edo State Police Command has confirmed the successful rescue of the remaining six passengers who were kidnapped during a violent attack along the Benin–Lagos Road. The victims were part of a group...

item.title: US changes green card rules requiring applicants to apply from outside the country
item.description: The US Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) has announced a major policy change that will require most foreign nationals already in the United States to leave the country and apply for permane...

item.title: Saba offers a rare, unspoiled Caribbean experience focused on nature, hiking and local culture
item.description: Saba, a small Dutch Caribbean island of just 13 square kilometres and about 2,100 residents, is presented as one of the region’s most distinctive and least commercialised destinations. Often called th...

item.title: NBDSA pulls up Zee News over unverified broadcasts linked to Muslims and communal claims
item.description: The News Broadcasting and Digital Standards Authority (NBDSA) has censured Zee News for airing allegedly misleading and communally sensitive programmes that relied on unverified claims and speculation...

item.title: Tanzania enforces restrictions on foreign participation in locally reserved business sectors
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has stepped up enforcement measures aimed at protecting business sectors that are officially reserved for Tanzanian citizens. The move is part of a wider strategy to ensure ...

item.title: GMA News ranks 3rd globally in Tubular Labs April 2026 digital video news rankings
item.description: GMA News has achieved a major milestone in global digital media performance after ranking No. 3 worldwide in the Tubular Labs Leadership Rankings for News and Politics for April 2026. The Philippine n...

item.title: South African education department warns public about fake ‘Teacher Assistant Phase 6’ job advertisements circulating on Facebook
item.description: South Africa’s Department of Basic Education has issued a warning to the public about fraudulent social media posts advertising supposed ‘Teacher Assistants Phase 6’ job opportunities for 2026. These ...

item.title: How a Canadian province could legally pursue separation under the Clarity Act
item.description: The article explains the legal framework governing whether a Canadian province can unilaterally secede from the country. It highlights that, according to a 1998 Supreme Court of Canada reference case ...

item.title: Tanzania outlines measures to manage fuel price increases amid global oil market tensions
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has announced a series of measures aimed at reducing the impact of rising fuel prices caused by ongoing tensions in the Gulf region and disruptions in global oil markets. Th...

item.title: Filipina-Japanese singer-songwriter ena mori shares creative process and musical inspirations
item.description: Filipina-Japanese singer-songwriter ena mori is highlighted as one of the most promising emerging acts in the Philippine music scene, known for her synth-heavy indie pop sound and growing internationa...

item.title: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa govt declares four public holidays for Eidul Azha from May 26 to 29
item.description: The Khyber Pakhtunkhwa government has officially announced four public holidays for Eidul Azha from May 26 to May 29, covering Tuesday to Friday. The decision was shared through an official notificati...

item.title: Delhi Police Submit Fourth Supplementary Chargesheet in Parliament Security Breach Case Under UAPA
item.description: The Delhi Police Special Cell has filed a fourth supplementary chargesheet of nearly 14,000 pages in connection with the 2023 Parliament security breach case. The chargesheet was submitted before the ...

item.title: Two tourists found dead near river in Kruger National Park after search operation
item.description: Two tourists have been found dead in the Kruger National Park in what authorities have described as a serious and unusual incident. According to South African National Parks (SANParks), the bodies wer...

item.title: A Look at Rosemary Clooney’s Career and Fame Through 1950s Photographs
item.description: The article from Vintage Everyday revisits the life and career of American singer and actress Rosemary Clooney through a collection of photographs from the 1950s. Clooney rose to prominence during the...

item.title: Ododo and Yahaya Bello commend transparency after voting in APC presidential primaries in Kogi
item.description: Governor of Kogi State, Alhaji Ahmed Ododo, alongside former governor Yahaya Bello, participated in the All Progressives Congress (APC) presidential primaries held in Kogi State. After casting his vot...

item.title: Tanzania approves 2.5 trillion/- budget to accelerate road and bridge infrastructure projects
item.description: Tanzania’s National Assembly has approved a 2.5 trillion shilling budget for the Ministry of Works, marking a major step in strengthening the country’s transport infrastructure. The funding will mainl...

item.title: Tanzania Seeks Rio Ferdinand’s Support to Attract International Investors
item.description: Tanzania has approached former England and Manchester United captain Rio Ferdinand to help promote the country’s investment opportunities to international business communities. The request was made by...

item.title: The Boys finale explained: Homelander is finally defeated in the final season conclusion
item.description: The fifth and final season of The Boys concludes with its eighth episode, ‘Blood And Bone’, bringing the long-running Prime Video series to a definitive end. The story, overseen by showrunner Eric Kri...

item.title: Thomas Ntuli rejects Joe Sibanyoni’s loan claims and says payments were linked to alleged extortion
item.description: Businessman Thomas Ntuli has denied claims by Mpumalanga taxi boss Joe “Ferrari” Sibanyoni that he borrowed R900,000 from him. Sibanyoni made the allegation in a bail application linked to an extortio...
